summary: Historical predicate identity for the Stable built-in pointwise logical family. It preserves packed known-width integer and finite bitfield/#flags behavior and additionally admits exact same-shape NumericArray operands over one exact known-width integer element domain. Scalar Bool, NumericArray<Bool>, generic collections, dynamic/heterogeneous shapes, implicit broadcast or conversion, user-defined carriers, and result-as-control use reject deterministically. Binary operands evaluate left-to-right once with zero short circuit or flow narrowing.
requires: packed route admits one exact known-width integer domain or one identical finite bitfield/#flags domain and preserves that domain
shaped route admits only NumericArray<I, rank R, shape S, orientation O> where I is one exact known-width integer domain and all binary operands have exactly I/R/S/O
binary operands evaluate left-to-right once; unary complement evaluates its operand once
short_circuit_count and flow_narrowing_fact_count are zero
implicit broadcast, shape conversion, element conversion, width conversion, signedness conversion, Trait/witness/provider lookup, and runtime fallback counts are zero
scalar Bool, NumericArray<Bool>, generic collections, dynamic/heterogeneous shapes, and arbitrary user carriers are not admitted
packed results are never Bool and a shaped result preserves exact element domain, rank, shape, and orientation
preconditions: operator token is exactly binary &&, ||, ^^ or prefix ~~
packed route supplies one exact known-width integer or identical finite bitfield/#flags identity
shaped route supplies NumericArray with statically exact integer element domain, rank, shape, and orientation
no user-defined glyph dispatch, runtime lookup, implicit conversion, or broadcast is attempted
normalization: classify packed versus shaped carrier; normalize exact integer signedness/width or finite bitfield/#flags identity; for NumericArray normalize exact element domain, rank, shape, orientation, and visible result-owner responsibility before decision
decision_procedure: validate the closed RCTS-V5 descriptor
classify scalar Bool, packed, NumericArray, generic collection, and unsupported/user-defined carrier before any dispatch lookup
on the shaped route require a current numeric element domain, then exact rank/shape/orientation, then exact element width/signedness/domain in that order
evaluate BitwiseDomainAdmitted diagnostic_dispatch conditions once in their declared deterministic order
on success fix the intrinsic operation and exact result carrier before MIR construction; evaluate binary operands left-to-right once or the unary operand once

emit only the diagnostic selected by the first matching condition, never the entire secondary_diagnostics list; otherwise return success with zero short circuit, flow narrowing, implicit conversion, broadcast, witness lookup, or runtime fallback
termination_metric: finite descriptor size plus strictly decreasing dependency DAG rank
evaluation_order: preconditions -> normalization -> dependencies -> local rule -> diagnostic
